verdict_reasoning: BAX is a pro-apoptotic BCL2 family protein that is predominantly cytosolic in healthy cells and translocates to the mitochondrial outer membrane upon apoptotic stimulus. There is no evidence of plasma membrane localization or surface accessibility from the extracellular face. The protein's C-terminal tail anchor directs it exclusively to mitochondria, not to the cell surface. BAX functions as an intracellular regulator of apoptosis via mitochondrial membrane permeabilization, with no role in direct extracellular binder engagement.
